headline: Consumer Discretionary Stocks’ Long Winter Isn’t Over
summary: Everything seems to be going well on Wall Street—but consumer stocks aren’t joining the party.  Corporate earnings are booming, on pace for another quarter of double-digit percentage gains as the reporting season draws to a close.  The State Street Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR exchange-traded fund is down more than 1% since the start of the year—compared with gains of more than 8% and 15% for the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq, respectively.

headline: McDonald’s Looks Undervalued Despite Stock Pressure This Year
summary: Let me cut to the chase. McDonald’s (NYSE:MCD) just posted a clean Q1 2026 beat, but the stock is trading near its 52-week low and roughly 1% below its 52-week high only because of how this past year played out. Our 24/7 Wall St. price target for McDonald’s is $329.64 over the next 12 months, ... McDonald’s Looks Undervalued Despite Stock Pressure This Year

headline: The fast food stocks benefitting from value, premium offerings
summary: Fast food companies reported strong first quarter earnings, their respective chief executives emphasizing the value being offered to consumers amid rising food costs, oil (CL=F, BZ=F) and gas prices (RB=F), and overall costs of living. Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi brings on Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré and B. Riley Wealth chief market strategist Art Hogan to talk more about the performance of fast food stocks.

headline: McDonald’s CEO Makes Stunning 1,000 Times More Than Workers
summary: The SEC requires companies to show the relationship between the CEO’s pay and the median pay of the company’s workers. For McDonald’s (NYSE MCD), that figure is 1,082 to 1, which is among the highest in the S&P 500. To do the math, CEO Chris Kempczinski made $20,574,525 in 2025. The median pay of all ... McDonald’s CEO Makes Stunning 1,000 Times More Than Workers

headline: Brokers Suggest Investing in McDonald's (MCD): Read This Before Placing a Bet
summary: The average brokerage recommendation (ABR) for McDonald's (MCD) is equivalent to a Buy. The overly optimistic recommendations of Wall Street analysts make the effectiveness of this highly sought-after metric questionable. So, is it worth buying the stock?

headline: MCD Q1 Deep Dive: Menu Innovation, Value Focus, and Global Expansion Shape Outlook
summary: Fast-food chain McDonald’s (NYSE:MCD) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 9.4% year on year to $6.52 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.83 per share was 3.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Food Safety Video Puts McDonald's Reputation And Valuation In Focus
summary: A viral video shows a McDonald's employee allegedly contaminating food at a Southbridge, Massachusetts restaurant. Local police and health authorities are investigating the incident and related food safety practices at the location. McDonald's, listed as NYSE:MCD, faces renewed public scrutiny over its food handling standards following the footage. For investors tracking NYSE:MCD, this incident sits against a backdrop of a share price at $274.6 and a mixed set of recent returns. The stock...
